summary: Iteration 99 — Backend testing of Caisse Product Approval Workflow + Deduplication. 14/14 tests passed (100%). All requested scenarios validated: pending workflow (manager → pending, admin → approved), filters (include_pending / status=all / status=pending), dedicated /caisse/products/pending endpoint (correctly routed before /{id}), approve idempotency, reject (deletion + audit trace), duplicates detection by normalized name (lower + sans accents + espaces collapsés), deduplicate with dry_run + real run keeping the product with most history, and verification that pending products are NOT exposed by default GET (cannot be used in billing).
critical_code_review_comments: routers/products.py is clean (well-scoped, async, no _id leakage via projection {_id:0}). OK.

Route ordering verified: api_router.include_router(caisse_products_router) at line 200 runs BEFORE the inline /caisse/products/{product_id} route at line 3315 of server.py — so GET /caisse/products/pending is correctly matched by the router and not captured as {id}='pending'. Confirmed by test_pending_endpoint_returns_pending_only.
Helper _count_history in routers/products.py uses both items.product_id AND items.name OR items.description across invoices & expenses — robust against legacy data without product_id.
Sort tie-breaker after history count is created_at ascending — kept product is the OLDEST when history is tied. Matches expectation (3c).
Idempotency of approve: returns {success:true, already_approved:true} on second call (HTTP 200). Verified.
Reject correctly deletes and inserts a row in caisse_products_rejections (audit). Verified via 404 on subsequent approve attempt.

Suggestion (non-blocking): the inline POST /caisse/products in server.py reads role from query params (modified_by_role). Frontend must reliably pass this — there's no server-side auth check. If a manager UI omits modified_by_role, the product would default to non-admin (pending) — that's safe. But if the frontend mistakenly passes 'admin', any client could bypass. Consider deriving the role from an authenticated session/token in a future hardening pass.

Suggestion (non-blocking): /caisse/products/deduplicate has no role gate. Anyone reaching the API can wipe duplicates. Acceptable for an admin-only operation behind frontend, but a server-side check (modified_by_role=='admin') would be safer.
test_credentials: admin / Caisse2026 ; Mères AHOUANDJINOU / PIN 2468 (not used directly — endpoints accept modified_by/modified_by_role as query params, no auth header).
seed_data_creation: Created ~9 products with prefix TEST_DEDUP_<rand6>_* for the workflow + 3 normalized duplicates (Café Express variants). Automatic cleanup fixture removes any remaining TEST_DEDUP_* products at end of session.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file /app/backend/tests/test_caisse_product_workflow_iter99.py is self-cleaning (deletes TEST_DEDUP_* at session end). It loads REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env directly since the env var isn't exported in the pytest runtime. All 14 tests passed in 1.74s. Frontend was NOT tested per explicit instruction.
